retry_reason: A put wrapper releases a reference; retrying would cause a double-put leading to use-after-free.
contract_summary: This function releases one reference on an afs_wb_key object. When the refcount reaches zero, it cleans up by releasing the underlying struct key (key_put) and freeing the wbk structure itself. The caller must not use the wbk pointer after calling this function, as the object may be freed. Passing NULL is safe and results in a no-op. This is the standard lifecycle end for afs_wb_key objects.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to decrement the refcount of an afs_wb_key and release the associated struct key and free the wbk when last reference is dropped. Only refcount management and paired cleanup.

check.reason: refcount_dec_and_test(&wbk->usage) decrements wbk->usage, which is of type refcount_t. The access path from parameter wbk through usage to refs matches the callee's member-access path r.refs. Type chain afs_wb_key--refcount_t--atomic_t is consistent.
